Arjun Patel
Senior Data Engineer — Streaming Infrastructure
☎ +44 20 7946 0958 ✉ arjun.patel@gmail.com in/arjun-patel-data-eng gh/ArjunPatel https://arjun.dev

stateful processing, cutting infrastructure costs by $2.1M
for new pipeline development by 70% compared to raw Flink
annually and improving metric freshness from 5min to <500ms

for 97% of ingestion paths

Software Engineer — Data Infrastructure EXTRACURRICULAR
Developed internal streaming data platform using Apache Kafka
and Apache Spark; onboarded 12 cross-functional teams and Apache Kafka Community | 2020-Present
Contributor & KIP Review Committee Member
reduced data pipeline development time from 3 weeks to 3 days
Contributed 12 pull requests merged into core Kafka codebase,
via reusable abstractions
including KIP-694 (transaction coordinator improvements);
Designed schema evolution strategy for event streams
reviews averaged 3.2 PRs/week across 8+ KIPs affecting
supporting 30+ event types with backward/forward compatibility;
millions of users
reduced production incidents related to schema mismatches by
Co-authored technical RFC for stream recovery optimization
100% (from 8/quarter to 0)
adopted by Confluent engineering; improvements reduce
Authored technical blog posts and internal documentation on
bootstrap time by 40% for 10TB+ clusters
stream processing patterns; reached 45K+ readers and
contributed to 4 tech talks at company conferences
Data Engineering Europe Conference | 2021-2023 SKILLS
Speaker & Workshop Organizer
Delivered 3 keynote talks on 'Streaming at Scale' reaching
Languages: Python, Kotlin, Go, SQL, Scala, Java
1,200+ attendees at conferences in Berlin, Amsterdam, London;
talks averaged 4.8/5.0 rating with 80K+ YouTube views Technologies: Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, Spark Streaming,
combined Apache Beam, Debezium, Airflow, ClickHouse, PostgreSQL,
Redis, AWS (S3, Kinesis, EC2), GCP (BigQuery, Dataflow)
Designed and led 2-day hands-on workshop on production
Kafka architectures; mentored 60+ engineers; 92% completion
Tools: Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, Git, Prometheus, Grafana,
rate with post-workshop survey showing 8.9/10 satisfaction DataDog, Jira, Confluence
